what_it_does: Implements a browser-local memory and preference store (MS_MEMORY) with a deterministic token-bag embedding stand-in, cosine recall, weighted origin tracking, localStorage persistence, pub/sub reactivity, and a React hook.
observations: Embedding is a 24-dimensional deterministic unit vector built by hashing each lowercase alphanumeric token into a bucket and normalizing.
Recall scores memories by cosine similarity multiplied by an item weight and supports optional topic filtering.
Memories carry an origin tag from a fixed taxonomy: stated, observed, approved, rejected, imported, derived.
Persistence stores content, topic, weight, origin, scope, source_ref, and timestamps, but recomputes embeddings on load to keep storage small.
setPreference both stores a key/value pair and learns a weighted stated memory recording the preference.
A seed set of approved/rejected design memories is loaded when no persisted data exists.
useMemoryStore subscribes to MS_MEMORY and forces a React re-render via useState.
Subscriber exceptions in notify are silently swallowed.
standouts: Deterministic hashed-token embedding used as a zero-dependency semantic recall stand-in.
Every memory is annotated with origin and weight, making the memory trail auditable.
Preference changes are automatically learned as stated memories with elevated weight.
Seed data encodes a project-specific design canon.
conventions: IIFE attached to window for global access; React hook is a separate window function.
localStorage JSON serialization with a versioned key (ms_memory_v1).
Default option handling via opts || {} and nullish coalescing for weights.
Console error logging for malformed input rather than throwing.
Internal arrays exposed via _items and _prefs for debugging.
concerns: The 24-bucket hash embedding is a very coarse semantic proxy and will collide unrelated tokens.
localStorage and notify errors are caught and ignored, which can hide failures.
No validation of content length, id collisions, or scope values.
Weight clamping range 0.1-3 is arbitrary and not configurable.
useMemoryStore assumes React is globally available and is not SSR-safe.
notes: A self-contained, prototype-grade memory substrate that trades embedding quality for zero dependencies while keeping a stable API for future local models.
questions: How is this browser store intended to synchronize with the db/schema.sql memory and preference tables?
What is the expected format and usage of source_ref?
How should duplicate or conflicting memories with the same topic/content be reconciled?
Is scope intended for access control, project scoping, or filtering, and what values are valid?
What is the intended lifecycle for rejected memories versus approved ones?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is described as a local-embedding, auditable memory store and it implements exactly that, minus production database integration.
apparent_themes: Auditable provenance through origin and weight
Design canon encoded as retrievable memory
Stable API surface with swappable implementation
intention_signals: To give the studio a recallable, editable memory of design decisions and reviewer preferences that can run without external services today while remaining API-compatible with a real local embedding model later.
novelty: A deterministic FNV-style token-bag embedding used as a semantic-recall stand-in, and the automatic conversion of every preference update into a weighted stated memory.
contribution: A self-contained memory substrate with learn/recall/preference APIs, deterministic embedding, local persistence, subscription reactivity, and a seeded design canon; a fused system would keep the API shape, the origin/weight audit model, and the pattern of persisting content while recomputing embeddings.
summary_for_embedding: memory.js implements a browser-local memory store (MS_MEMORY) with deterministic token-bag embeddings, cosine recall, weighted origins, preference storage, localStorage persistence, and a React subscription hook.
intention_for_embedding: to provide a dependency-free, prototype-grade repository memory for design decisions and user preferences with a stable API surface for later local embedding models.

symbols.does: Computes a deterministic 24-dimensional unit vector from a text string by hashing its lowercase tokens into buckets.
symbols.description: embed tokenizes the input, hashes each token with an FNV-like loop, accumulates counts into a 24-slot vector, and normalizes it to unit length. It is intended as a zero-dependency stand-in for a real local embedding model.

symbols.does: Returns the cosine similarity between two equal-length vectors.
symbols.description: cosine computes the dot product of two 24-dimensional vectors. Because both vectors are normalized, the result is the cosine similarity used to rank recall results.

symbols.does: Registers a callback to be called on memory changes and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: subscribe adds a function to an internal Set and returns a closure that removes it, enabling reactive consumers such as the React hook to update when the store mutates.

symbols.does: Calls every subscriber callback, swallowing exceptions.
symbols.description: notify iterates over registered subscribers and invokes each one inside a try/catch so a single failing subscriber does not break the notification loop.

symbols.does: Serializes memory content and preferences to localStorage under ms_memory_v1.
symbols.description: persist writes ids, origins, topics, content, weights, scope, source_ref, timestamps, and preferences to localStorage, omitting embeddings so they can be recomputed on load. It then notifies subscribers.

symbols.does: Creates and stores a new memory item with an embedding, origin, topic, and weight.
symbols.description: learn validates that content exists, assigns defaults for origin/topic/weight/scope, computes an embedding from topic plus content, pushes the item into the internal array, and persists unless marked quiet. It returns the created item.

symbols.does: Removes a memory item by id and persists the store.
symbols.description: forget finds the first item with the matching id, removes it from the items array, and calls persist so the deletion is reflected in localStorage and subscribers.

symbols.does: Clamps and updates a memory item's weight, then persists.
symbols.description: setWeight looks up the item by id, clamps the new weight between 0.1 and 3.0, assigns it, and persists so recall scoring changes are saved.

symbols.does: Applies a patch to a memory item and recomputes its embedding if content or topic changed.
symbols.description: update merges the provided patch into the matching memory, refreshes the embedding when topic or content is patched, and persists the updated store.

symbols.does: Returns the top-k memories ranked by weighted cosine similarity to a query.
symbols.description: recall embeds the query, optionally filters by topic, scores each memory as cosine similarity times item weight, drops zero scores, sorts descending, and slices to k results.

symbols.does: Retrieves a stored preference value by key.
symbols.description: preference simply returns the value from the internal prefs object, or undefined if the key has not been set.

symbols.does: Stores a key/value preference and learns it as a stated memory.
symbols.description: setPreference writes the value into prefs, then calls learn with origin 'stated', topic 'preference', and an elevated weight of 1.4 so the preference is also recallable as memory.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of all memory items.
symbols.description: all returns items.slice() so callers get an array copy rather than the internal array reference.

symbols.does: Returns the distinct non-null topics currently stored.
symbols.description: topics maps items to their topic, filters out null/undefined values, and returns a deduplicated array of topic strings.

symbols.does: Loads an initial set of project design memories when no persisted store exists.
symbols.description: seed creates a handful of approved, rejected, stated, and observed memories covering style, layout, voice, color, and motion, using _quiet to avoid redundant persistence calls during initialization.
